camera.notes: Orthographic package smoke camera over the cooked 2D scene.

inputMap.purpose: Move the player sprite through deterministic 2D gameplay smoke rows.
Exercise audio, contact, and animation feedback in the package proof.

coreLoop.objective: Move the cooked player sprite through the packaged scene while package-visible systems report deterministic gameplay counters.
coreLoop.feedback: Sprite animation, HUD submission, audio cue, physics contacts, navigation, AI, and simulation counters are emitted by selected package recipes.
coreLoop.winState: Installed package recipes report clean 2D package, gameplay systems, native sprite, and simulation evidence.
coreLoop.failState: Missing package files, invalid scene references, or unsupported broad claims fail schema/static/package validation.
coreLoop.restart: Rerun the package smoke from the manifest-derived runtime package files without mutating cooked payloads.

sceneList.purpose: Desktop runtime configuration shipped through runtimePackageFiles.
Package index used by installed validation and runtime scene loading.

assetRequests.purpose: Reviewed RGBA8 source frames feed first-party sprite atlas authoring before cooked runtime consumption.
First-party tilemap metadata drives deterministic visible tile counters.
Cooked audio cue proves device-independent audio feedback.

qualityGates.evidence: Installed package validation loads manifest-derived config, package index, and cooked scene payloads.
Gameplay systems, native sprite, UI, audio, physics, navigation, and AI counters are selected package evidence.
Simulation orchestration smoke reports fixed-step command playback and clean diagnostics without running broad game rules.

gameplayContract.scene: GameEngine.Scene.v1 loaded from a cooked package and validated by mirakana::validate_playable_2d_scene
gameplayContract.audio: mirakana::AudioMixer using a cooked audio payload
gameplayContract.physics: mirakana::PhysicsWorld2D deterministic contact and trigger counters
gameplayContract.renderer: mirakana::IRenderer from the desktop host with deterministic NullRenderer fallback or host-owned RHI-backed presentation when packaged shader artifacts are present
gameplayContract.currentRuntime: host-gated SDL3 desktop runtime package proof for 2D gameplay. D3D12 package window smoke uses packaged DXIL shader bytecode and --require-d3d12-shaders --require-d3d12-renderer through host-owned presentation; the native 2D sprite package proof adds --require-native-2d-sprites so cooked scene sprite texture/material identity, atlas-backed scene sprite planning counters (sprite_batch_plan_atlas_backed_batches, sprite_batch_plan_repeated_atlas_batches, sprite_batch_plan_repeated_atlas_sprites), and HUD submission flow through the host-owned native RHI sprite overlay path with renderer-owned native sprite batch execution counters. The sprite atlas source authoring target records reviewed RGBA8 frame rows for plan_sprite_atlas_source_authoring, emits GameEngine.TextureSource.v1 plus GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry.v1 authoring files, and keeps those source files outside runtimePackageFiles before cooked runtime consumption. The sprite animation package proof adds a first-party cooked sprite_animation payload and --require-sprite-animation so deterministic flipbook ticks through advance_runtime_sprite_flipbook emit sprite_flipbook_frames_sampled and sprite_flipbook_frames_applied counters before sprite frame application emits sprite_animation_frames_sampled and sprite_animation_frames_applied counters. The tilemap runtime UX proof uses first-party GameEngine.Tilemap.v1 metadata and --require-tilemap-runtime-ux so visible tile cells emit tilemap_cells_sampled and tilemap_diagnostics counters without claiming runtime image decoding, production atlas packing, or full tilemap editor UX. The gameplay systems package proof uses --require-gameplay-systems and --require-procedural-generation so 2D PhysicsWorld contacts/triggers, NavigationGrid path/agent movement, AI perception blackboard, behavior authoring validation, quest/dialogue state progression, quest/dialogue save-state validation, runtime inventory item pickup/state validation/recipe completion, reviewed construction placement validation plus runtime-scene placement intent planning, reviewed procedural generation plus procedural placement intent planning, and behavior tree counters emit gameplay_systems_* fields including gameplay_systems_behavior_authoring_diagnostics, gameplay_systems_behavior_authoring_trace_nodes, gameplay_systems_quest_dialogue_transition_rows, gameplay_systems_quest_dialogue_action_ids, gameplay_systems_quest_dialogue_reward_ids, gameplay_systems_quest_dialogue_state_rows, gameplay_systems_inventory_items_transition_rows, gameplay_systems_inventory_items_final_workbench_quantity, gameplay_systems_construction_placement_validation_rows, gameplay_systems_construction_placement_intent_rows, gameplay_systems_construction_placement_intent_accepted_rows, gameplay_systems_construction_placement_intent_occupied_cells, gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_rows, gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_object_rows, gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_encounter_rows, gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_loot_rows, gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_replay_hash, and gameplay_systems_procedural_generation_package_visible_rows. The world-region streaming package proof uses --require-world-region-streaming so reviewed package candidates drive deterministic region load/keep/unload safe-point rows with budget evidence, missing-region diagnostics, reviewed package adoption counters, and clean safe-point diagnostics through world_region_streaming_* fields. The entity scale/culling package proof uses --require-entity-scale-culling so plan_runtime_entity_scale_culling emits deterministic package-visible entity_scale_culling_* counters for planned rows, visible/culled rows, LOD rows, update buckets, projected draw/update costs, budget-protected rows, clean diagnostics, and budget diagnostic evidence without renderer/RHI mutation. The scripting sandbox policy proof uses --require-scripting-sandbox-policy so plan_runtime_script_sandbox emits deterministic scripting_sandbox_* counters for reviewed entrypoint rows, allowed permission rows, denied default-unsafe permission diagnostics, unsupported host API diagnostics, budget rows, replay seed evidence, and clean reviewed-policy diagnostics without interpreting scripts, loading code, opening files, networking, spawning processes, binding native plugins, mutating packages, or exposing native handles. The networking foundation policy proof uses --require-networking-foundation-policy so plan_runtime_network_foundation emits deterministic networking_foundation_* counters for reviewed session rows, transport rows, replication channel rows, replay prerequisite rows, rejected unsafe transport requirements, insecure remote transport diagnostics, and clean reviewed-policy diagnostics without opening sockets, adding transport middleware, performing encryption/authentication handshakes, matchmaking, rollback/prediction, NAT traversal, package mutation, or native handle exposure. The simulation orchestration package proof uses --require-simulation-orchestration so plan_runtime_simulation_orchestration emits deterministic simulation_orchestration_* counters for planned fixed-step rows, ordered command playback rows, budget-limited frames, invalid command diagnostics, consumed/remaining time, and clean reviewed-loop diagnostics without running game rules, pacing renderer frames, implementing rollback/lockstep, mutating packages, or exposing native handles. The gameplay authoring review package proof uses --require-gameplay-authoring-review so review_gameplay_authoring_request emits deterministic gameplay_authoring_review_* counters for accepted feature rows, mutation ledger rows, remediation rows, unsupported capability/recipe/package evidence diagnostics, and unsupported broad-authoring claim diagnostics without applying fixes, mutating cooked packages, executing commands, or claiming autonomous commercial game design. Vulkan package window smoke uses toolchain/host-gated SPIR-V shader bytecode and --require-vulkan-shaders --require-vulkan-renderer. public native or RHI handle access remains unsupported, broad production sprite batching readiness remains unsupported, broad background package streaming remains unsupported, broad entity scale execution/performance/GPU culling remains unsupported, broad scripting/mod runtime execution remains unsupported, broad multiplayer/networking readiness remains unsupported, broad deterministic multiplayer readiness remains unsupported, autonomous commercial game design remains unsupported, and general production renderer quality remains unsupported.

backendReadiness.graphics: NullRenderer fallback plus host-gated D3D12 package window smoke, native 2D sprite package proof with atlas-backed scene sprite plan counters and renderer-owned native sprite batch execution counters, and Vulkan package window smoke through packaged shader artifacts. Sprite atlas source authoring remains a reviewed tooling/source contract only; runtime image decoding, renderer/RHI residency from source images, public native or RHI handle access, broad production sprite batching readiness, and general production renderer quality remain unsupported.
backendReadiness.audio: device-independent cooked audio payload mixed through mirakana_audio
backendReadiness.physics: MK_physics 2D deterministic contact and trigger proof through --require-gameplay-systems
backendReadiness.navigation: MK_navigation grid path planning and agent update proof through --require-gameplay-systems
backendReadiness.ai: MK_ai behavior authoring, perception-blackboard, and behavior-tree proof plus MK_runtime quest/dialogue progression, inventory items crafting, construction placement validation, procedural generation, entity scale/culling package evidence, scripting sandbox policy package evidence, networking foundation policy package evidence, simulation orchestration package evidence, MK_tools gameplay authoring review package evidence, and MK_runtime_scene placement/procedural-placement intent proof through --require-gameplay-systems, --require-procedural-generation, --require-entity-scale-culling, --require-scripting-sandbox-policy, --require-networking-foundation-policy, --require-simulation-orchestration, and --require-gameplay-authoring-review
